notes:
STRUCTURE (re-verified): Standard SM10 set. Per official Pokemon Center listing, each pack = 10 game cards + 1 basic Energy (Energy not modeled, no value). ThePriceDex independently reports "11 cards per booster" = the same 10+1. Booster Display Box = 36 packs (Pokemon Center product 169-81547, confirmed via WebSearch). ETB = 8 packs (Pokemon Center product 169-80563 + ToyWiz/TCGplayer descriptions). Slots: 4 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo (filler), 1 rare-or-better hit slot.

HIT SLOT SOURCE (re-fetched, figures confirmed identical on two independent prompts): ThePriceDex sm10 partition as 1-in-X: Rare non-holo 1:1.5 (0.66667), Rare Holo 1:6 (0.16667), Rare Holo GX 1:10 (0.10000), Ultra Rare/full-art 1:24.2 (0.04132), Rainbow 1:56.5 (0.01770), Secret/gold 1:131.9 (0.00758). Raw sum = 0.99994. Corroborated by Flipside Gaming SM-era ranges (re-fetched): GX ~1:9, UR/full-art 1:18-36 (our 1:24.2 fits squarely), secret ~1:2 boxes.

COARSE-BUCKET MAPPING (engine: GX+full-art-Pokemon+full-art-Trainer => ultra-rare; rainbow+gold+secret => secret-rare; holo & non-holo rares => rare):
- rare = 0.66667+0.16667 = 0.83334
- ultra-rare = 0.10000+0.04132 = 0.14132
- secret-rare = 0.01770+0.00758 = 0.02528
Normalized to exactly 1.0: rare 0.8334, ultra-rare 0.1413, secret-rare 0.0253.

REVERSE-HOLO filler split by C/U pool counts (62 common : 53 uncommon): 62/115=0.539, 53/115=0.461.

CONFIDENCE = medium: both hit-rate sources re-fetched successfully and are mutually consistent, but ThePriceDex explicitly states rates are "estimates ... based on community data" with no disclosed sample size, so not "high".
